on the local cache for the `tenex-edge` project -- what channels do you see available?
Listed available NIP-29 channels for tenex-edge and noted that #29er, while visible on the relay, doesn't appear in the local owned_groups cache.
Explained owned_groups as a local routing table tracking NIP-29 groups created by this daemon instance, distinct from project_meta which caches group metadata from the relay.

that's a stupid design -- if a the group was not created by this backend it doesn't mean it's not a fucking admin -- obviously -- so whether it can manage it or not depends on whether the RELAY shows this backend as a fucking admin (which I already said should be the fucking source of truth, yet you keep insisting in adding hacks and bypassing the source of fucking truth!!!!) also -- #29er WAS fucking created by this backend!!!

Agreed the relay's admin list is the authoritative source of truth, identified that owns_group conflates 'daemon-created' with 'is admin' causing three bugs, and proposed fixing it by replacing owns_group checks with relay-based admin queries and fetching live group metadata on cache misses.
